gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/172)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/172)
text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى أَعَارَ أَعْدَاءَهُ أَخْلَاقاً مِنْ أَخْلَاقِ أَوْلِيَائِهِ لِيَعِيشَ أَوْلِيَاؤُهُ مَعَ أَعْدَائِهِ فِي دَوْلَاتِهِمْ .
وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى وَ لَوْ لَا ذَلِكَ لَمَا تَرَكُوا وَلِيّاً لِلَّهِ إِلَّا قَتَلُوهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Abdullah Al Hajjal, from Abu Usman Al Qabousy, from the one who mentioned,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Blessed and High has Lent to His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> enemies the manners from the mannerisms of His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> friends, so that His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> friends can live along with His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> enemies in their governments’.

And in another report, ‘(He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said): ‘And had it not been for that, they would not have left a (single) friend of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, except that they would have killed him’.
en.sarwar: 13. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from ‘Abd Allah al-Hajjall from abu ‘Uthman al-Qabusi from the people whom he has mentioned (in his book) from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High, has, of the moral behavior of His friends, lent to His enemies so that His friends can live with His enemies in their governments.’” In another Hadith it is stated that had it not been as such they would not have left any of Allah’s friends alive.
